name: Cable Wrist Curl
resistance_profile: A low-pulley cable keeps constant tension on the wrist flexors from full stretch to full contraction, unlike free weights that unload near the top.
cues: Anchor the forearm so only the wrist moves
Pause and squeeze at the top
execution_steps: Set the cable to the lowest pulley and take the handle with an underhand, palm-up grip.
Rest the forearm along your thigh or a bench with the wrist just past the edge, palm up.
Let the cable pull the hand into wrist extension for a full stretch at the bottom.
Curl the handle up by flexing the wrist, squeeze, then lower under control.
good_when: You want constant tension on the wrist flexors through the whole range
You want a joint-friendly forearm option that keeps load on the stretch
bad_when: You are chasing maximal absolute load for the flexors
common_errors: Lifting the forearm off its support
Cutting the range short at the bottom
progressions: Add a pause at full stretch
regressions: Support the forearm fully on a bench
